capture.cameraPose: Desktop Chrome DevTools viewport after structured trace, hand-representation, and locomotion-attempt fields were added to the in-app Quest Evidence payload.
capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP selected http://localhost:5173/, reloaded the page, waited for Quest Evidence, verified zero console errors/warnings, then captured a viewport screenshot.

notes: The screenshot verifies evidence payload visibility, not clinical bay realism; actors, equipment, and scenario environment remain placeholder-quality in this flat preview.

The new evidence fields explicitly call out primitive or missing hand representation, but the visual scene still lacks production actors, mesh hands, clothing, skin, rigging, and detailed medical devices.
The Quest Evidence JSON is visible in the operator panel and includes traceInteractionAttempt, handRepresentationKind, and locomotionAttempt fields for structured follow-up.
The desktop capture shows the Copy Evidence path and structured payload, but it does not prove headset select latency, hand-pinch firing, or locomotion on physical Quest hardware.
The operator panel remains readable at the desktop viewport, with no obvious overlap between the evidence fields, trace controls, or runtime posture panel.

This artifact is desktop Chrome DevTools visual evidence only; it is not physical Quest proof, not immersive WebXR proof, not in-headset readability proof, not hand-tracking quality proof, and not production runtime readiness.
